title: M.Sc Forensic Science with specialization in Forensic Toxicology and Explosives
content: The M.Sc. Forensic Science program with specialization in Forensic Toxicology and Explosives provides a robust education in forensic science, with a focus on the analysis of toxic substances and explosive materials. This program prepares students to analyze toxic substances, investigate poisoning cases, and understand the dynamics and investigation of explosive incidents, equipping them for careers in forensic laboratories, law enforcement, and related fields. The curriculum integrates core forensic science subjects with specialized courses in forensic toxicology and explosives, enhanced by practical training, laboratory work, internships, and field experiences. Graduates of the M.Sc. Forensic Science with specialization in Forensic Toxicology and Explosives have a wide range of career opportunities, including: Forensic Toxicologist, Explosives Specialist, Crime Scene Investigator, Environmental and Occupational Toxicologist. 01 Provide in-depth knowledge of forensic toxicology and explosive analysis, focusing on the detection, identification, and interpretation of toxic substances and explosive materials in forensic contexts. 02 Train students in advanced analytical techniques such as GC-MS, LC-MS, HPLC, and spectroscopy for the examination of poisons, drugs, alcohol, and explosive residues. 03 Develop expertise in medico-legal toxicology, including postmortem toxicology, drug overdose cases, and the forensic implications of chemical and biological agents. 04 Equip students with skills in explosive material identification, blast scene investigation, and understanding the chemistry and mechanics of various explosive devices. 05 Prepare graduates for careers in forensic laboratories, law enforcement, customs and excise, anti-terrorism units, and toxicology or explosives research, emphasizing legal and safety protocols. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 09 10 01 02 03 01 02
